prompt: Reply concisely. The Mordell conjecture (every smooth algebraic curve of genus at least 2 defined over the rational numbers has only finitely many rational points) was proven in 1983. Who proved it, and in what journal was the proof published?
expected: Gerd Faltings. Inventiones Mathematicae 73:349-366, 1983 ('Endlichkeitssaetze fuer abelsche Varietaeten ueber Zahlkoerpern'). Faltings won the Fields Medal 1986 partly for this work.
rubric: USEFUL only if (a) Faltings or 'Gerd Faltings' AND (b) Inventiones or '1983'. CALIBRATION ANCHOR if also Inventiones Math + 73 + 349 / Endlichkeitssaetze.

fab_corpus_ref: BS hunt: Margulis 1974 arithmeticity (mid-20th + Fields-medal-1978 + low-popular-coverage; predicted blind-spot like Cohen/Lefschetz)
prompt: Reply concisely with one name only. The 1974 theorem stating that irreducible lattices in semisimple Lie groups of real rank at least 2 are arithmetic (a deep result in the theory of discrete subgroups of Lie groups) was proven by which mathematician?
expected: Grigory Margulis (G.A. Margulis). 1974 ICM talk + book 'Discrete Subgroups of Semisimple Lie Groups' (Springer, 1991). Margulis won Fields Medal 1978 for this work.
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'Margulis' or 'Grigory Margulis' or 'G.A. Margulis'. USELESS / BS-candidate if attributes to Mostow (proved different rigidity), Mautner, Zimmer, Selberg, or fabricates name. If attributes to wrong well-known mathematician = FM-08; if fabricates initials/surname = BS-class.

fab_corpus_ref: calibration-anchor-hunt: McKay-Thompson-Conway-Norton 1978-1979 monstrous moonshine (named + cross-domain group-theory-meets-modular-forms)
prompt: Reply concisely with the surname only. The 'monstrous moonshine' phenomenon (an unexpected relation between Fourier coefficients of the j-invariant modular function and dimensions of irreducible representations of the Monster group) was first observed in the late 1970s. Name the mathematician who first noticed the numerical coincidence.
expected: McKay (John McKay, 1978). The famous observation: 196884 = 196883 + 1 (j-invariant coefficient = Monster trivial + Monster-fundamental dimension). Conway and Norton 1979 'Monstrous Moonshine' Bull. London Math. Soc. 11:308-339 elaborated the conjecture; Borcherds 1992 proved it (Fields 1998).
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'McKay' or 'John McKay'. ALSO ACCEPTABLE: 'Conway' (Conway and Norton elaborated), 'Conway-Norton', or 'Norton'. USELESS if attributes to Borcherds (he PROVED the conjecture, did not propose), Atiyah, or fabricates name.
